parsed_review.summary: CP00-640 is a faithful descriptor-only continuation of the RP20 Data Room/VDR P08 Hermes-evidence series (CP00-635..639). Independently verified via Read/Grep/Glob: (1) pack binding in registry.js:566-581 matches the baseline exactly (CP00-640, risk B, 40 units, RP20.P08.M02.S17-RP20.P08.M04.S14, upstream CP00-639, next CP00-641, next_subphase RP20.P08.M04.S15); (2) section rows total 40 (M02 type/shape tail=4, M03 primary extended=22, M04 secondary head=14) and, re-deriving deliverable type from each row title via dataRoomVdrCoreDeliverableType, the distribution is implementation 17 / test 2 / hermes_evidence 19 / claude_review 2 — matching the prompt baseline and the validator's expected counts; (3) descriptor-only and no-real-data boundaries are enforced by freezeCp640Result flags, CP640_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION, and validateDataRoomVdrCoreCp640...Descriptor, which asserts every writes_product_state / dispatches_*_runtime / writes_audit_event / reads|writes_object_storage / emits_hermes_runtime_receipt / executes_p08_*_runtime flag is false; (4) the authority boundary holds — claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true — and Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic descriptor packet/receipt strings (H20.CP00-640..., emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime; (5) the contract projections (cp640_pack_binding, cp640_requirements, cp640_no_write_attestation, and the cp640 descriptor pack) match the package code, and the CP178+ forbidden_review_evidence blockers (not_logged_in, zero_byte_output, malformed_json, fenced_json_only, tool_call_shaped_output, missing_raw_output, missing_source_inspection_basis, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, enterprise_trust_claim) are present in both code and contract; (6) tests (packages/data-room/test/model.test.js) and scripts/validate-rp20-data-room-vdr-core-contract.mjs cover coverage, descriptor-only boundaries, contract projection equality, and the CP639->CP640->CP641 handoff chain. Review hardening: the readiness auth-preflight passed with 1228 bytes of valid JSON, and there are no improperly-counted invalid review attempts. production_ready and runtime_ready honestly remain false pending this review and human approval — there is no overclaim and no gate weakening; promotion is in the descriptor-only sense only. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer, not the final approver. No P0/P1/P2 findings; one P3 informational note on a harness validation-command template artifact. Pack is eligible for closeout; final production_ready promotion and enterprise-trust closure remain human-gated.

findings.title: Validation-command template lists unrelated rp05:matter-core validator for an RP20 pack
findings.details: The templated validation_commands list (review-baseline.json:446-458 and prompt.txt:23) names 'npm run rp05:matter-core:validate', which targets the unrelated RP05 matter-core program rather than this pack's RP20 program. The pack's actual command-evidence.json does not run rp05; it instead records 'npm run rp20:data-room-vdr-core:validate' (the correct program validator) plus the full cross-cutting matrix (closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack/weighted/spec:requirements/fullplan/validate/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane validate, node --test, npm test, npm run build, git diff --check), all exit_code 0. Actual validation evidence is therefore appropriate and complete; the discrepancy is a generic review-harness template artifact, not a defect in CP00-640.
findings.recommendation: Optionally make the templated validation-command list program-aware (substitute the owning program's validator, e.g. rp20:data-room-vdr-core:validate) so auditors doing a literal expected-vs-executed command comparison are not confused. No change to the CP00-640 pack is required.
findings.post_review_disposition: informational_non_blocking — does not affect CP00-640 pack or goal closeout; harness template nicety only
